mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining unemployment (UNRATE) and rising payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust economic activity that supports corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns despite GDPC1 growth > 2% in the prior 6-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signals improved corporate capacity utilization and lower financing costs, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns when INDPRO > 100 and FEDFUNDS < 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in inflation (CPIAUCSL) during a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) creates uncertainty that depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns despite CPIAUCSL > 2% YoY and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate improved business investment and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns when INDPRO > 100 and HOUST > 1200.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns despite RSAFS > 700k and HOUST > 1200.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) with stable payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a tight labor market that supports wage inflation and corporate pricing power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns when UNRATE < 4.1 and PAYEMS > 150k.
